Interpretation: The positive effect size means that females carrying the female-limited chromosome had higher fitness than females with the male-limited chromosome, when evolved and measured under benign conditions.

A positive effect size would that males carrying the male-limited chromosome had higher fitness than males with the female-limited chromosome, when evolved and measured under benign conditions (there is no difference though).
The positive effect size means that females carrying the female-limited chromosome had higher fitness than females with the male-limited chromosome, when evolved and measured under novel conditions.
The positive effect size means that males carrying the male-limited chromosome had higher fitness than males with the female-limited chromosome, when evolved and measured under novel conditions.
